message:
fix(ft-xbnl0.5.2): seed in_progress summary.json so e2e self-scenario passes artifact-contract

Closes a self-inflicted false-positive in the
`finish_line_verification_contract_shape` guard when it runs as part
of the e2e driver itself. The guard scans the latest run-directory per
scenario in `tests/e2e/logs/` and requires a parseable `summary.json`
with the contract keys present. The composer is invoked from inside
`tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_5_2_finish_line_guards.sh`, so the e2e
scenario's *own* still-running directory was being scanned — and at
the moment of the scan it had a `structured.log` but no `summary.json`
yet (the `write_summary` call only fires once a step resolves). The
guard correctly flagged it as `e2e_missing_summary_json_or_structured_log`,
which then bubbled up to a top-level `status: failed` in the composite
snapshot the previous run produced.

Scope:

1. tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_5_2_finish_line_guards.sh (+7 lines)

   Adds an early `write_summary "in_progress"` call right after the
   manifest existence check and before any guard work begins. This
   seeds a contract-shape-valid `summary.json` (scenario, outcome,
   artifact_dir, structured_log all populated) into the active run
   directory before the composition step runs, so the artifact-contract
   guard sees a satisfying record for the e2e scenario itself. The
   real per-step outcome still gets written via the existing
   `write_summary` calls when steps resolve, overwriting the
   `in_progress` placeholder. Inline comment explains the
   rationale so a future contributor isn't tempted to remove the
   call as redundant.

2. docs/ft-xbnl0-5-2-finish-line-guards-validation.json (+2/-2)

   Composite snapshot refreshed at 2026-04-16T05:58:32Z. The
   artifact-contract guard's `inspected` count rises 1 → 2 (the
   self-scenario directory now satisfies the contract instead of being
   flagged), `missing` stays empty, top-level `status: passed` stays
   green. No other guard outcomes moved.

3. docs/asupersync-cutover-runtime-guard-validation.json (+1/-1)
   docs/ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-validation.json (+1/-1)

   `checked_at` bumps only — the composer re-runs both sub-guards as
   part of its sweep, and the rest of each body is byte-identical.
   Strict proof-of-freshness, no policy or count changes.

Net effect: the e2e driver can now run cleanly under CI without the
artifact-contract guard tripping on its own in-flight bookkeeping.

files.patch:
@@ -114,6 +114,13 @@ if [[ ! -f "${MANIFEST}" ]]; then
   exit 1
 fi
 
+# Write an "in-progress" summary.json up front so the composition's
+# artifact-contract check (which runs inside the nominal composition step
+# below) does not flag our own still-running scenario directory as a
+# malformed finish-line bundle. The real outcome is overwritten via
+# write_summary when each step resolves.
+write_summary "in_progress"
+
 emit_log "preflight" "startup" "scenario_start" "started" "none" "none" "$(basename "${LOG_FILE}")"
 
 # Step 1: nominal run (cargo-test guard skipped to keep this scenario
